commands.eid.set: Set the Current Entity ID to %s.
commands.eid.increment: Incremented the Current Entity ID by %1$s. Current Entity ID: %2$s.
commands.eid.decrement: Decremented the Current Entity ID by %1$s. Current Entity ID: %2$s.
commands.eid.subscribe.true: %s subscribed to the Current Entity ID.
commands.eid.subscribe.false: %s unsubscribed from the Current Entity ID.
commands.eid.track.start.fail: You're already tracking the Current Entity ID!
commands.eid.track.stop: No longer tracking the Current Entity ID.
commands.eid.track.stop.fail: You're not tracking the Current Entity ID!
commands.eid.track:
Current Entity ID (%s min), total: %s, (%s/tick):
 - Overflow (start to finish) in %s ticks (%sh%sm%ss)
commands.eid.track.finished: Finished tracking the Current Entity ID.
commands.eid.fix.true: The Current Entity ID now behaves like §eMULTIPLAYER§r.
commands.eid.fix.false: The Current Entity ID now behaves like §eSINGLEPLAYER§r.
minihud.config.info_toggle.comment.infoC:
Show the server's ENTITY_COUNTER AtomicInteger
Note: This is only accurate with EIDTracker on the server,
or with Carpet TIS Addition and /log entityIdCounter.
Otherwise it is estimated on the client side,
based on the add entity packet, and it can
only detect entities within your render distance that way.
Enables separation of the client and server
ENTITY_COUNTER in single player.
This makes Entity IDs behave as they do in multiplayer.
